feature_costs.label: Core recording flow (state machine, speech, Claude, actions)
v2 features (history, tray, shortcuts, export, refine)
Visual design — dark glass, ambient glow (DESIGN-001)

at_a_glance: You have spent $8.24 across 15 sessions building Promptly from blank canvas to a shipped macOS app. Phase 2 was the heaviest investment at $4.01 across 8 sessions — the core recording flow, Claude integration, bug fixes, and visual design all shipped there. Phase 4 v2 features have been well-priced at $1.95 for React migration plus 8 new features. Session 15 was the lightest yet at $0.35 — a small cosmetic pass removing vibrancy bleed-through and adding an ambient glow.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: The kickoff session — brainstorm, architect, and spec review. Expensive per task but unavoidable; these decisions shaped everything that followed.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: Phase 1 foundation — 11 tasks including parallel subagents for the full Electron shell, IPC skeleton, and PATH resolution. Most expensive single session at $1.30.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: F-STATE planning session. Zero code tasks but produced the full spec, plan, and task breakdown for the state machine and UI skeleton.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: F-STATE build — 5 tasks completing the full state machine, all 6 panels, CSS tokens, and IPC wiring.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-005: F-FIRST-RUN + F-SPEECH planning. Two feature specs in one session — heavy reading day.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-006: Finishing FRN-004, the last first-run task. Short session, mostly a single-task cleanup.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-007: Design system implementation and 3 bug fixes. The L-sized design task dominated at ~$0.27.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-008: DECISION-004 (recording state moved into main window) plus BUG-004. Architecture refactor was the heavy lift.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-009: Vibrancy, BUG-007, and FEATURE-001 splash screen. Splash screen was the L task at ~$0.25.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-010: Phase 2 review + all 11 review fixes applied in one session. phase-2-review alone consumed ~$0.31.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-011: Final review gate cleared — 0 P0, 0 P1. Lean session; the app was already in good shape.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-012: v2 features day — history removal, language removal, and Phase 4 cleanup across 5 tasks.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-013: Minimal startup + cost tracking session. No implementation tasks; cheapest session at $0.165.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-014: FEATURE-011 pause/resume — full feature from spec to shipping in one session. vibe-add-feature planning consumed 33% ($0.225) of the $0.674 total.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-015: DESIGN-001 — vibrancy removed and ambient blue/purple glow added. The lightest session in the project at $0.35. One small targeted change to 4 source files, logged as D-008 and D-009.

recommendations.title: CLAUDE.md caching still disabled — 15 sessions, zero cache reads
recommendations.body: CLAUDE.md has grown significantly with active feature sections for F-SPEECH, F-CLAUDE, F-ACTIONS, FEATURE-004, FEATURE-007, FEATURE-009, and FEATURE-011 — all still live in the file even though those features are complete. It is now approximately 5,000 tokens and changes each session, preventing caching from ever activating. At this stage of the project, consider archiving the completed feature sections out of CLAUDE.md — the rules they enforced are now baked into the codebase. A leaner CLAUDE.md would cache reliably and save ~$0.10–0.15 per session.

forecast_narrative: The project is 97% complete — 3 tasks remain: manual Phase 3 smoke test, broader distribution prep (notarisation, Sparkle, landing page). These are S-sized tasks. Expect $0.10–0.25 to finish. Total project cost will land around $8.35–8.50, comfortably within budget for a solo macOS utility built end-to-end with Claude.
